headline: Clear ROI Data for Corporate Sponsors
subheading: Peerceptiv turns the real work inside your executive programs into structured skill evidence, giving sponsors ROI data they demand and your program a competitive advantage.
challengeHeader: The Market Has Shifted. Differentiate your Program.
challengeIntro: UNICON's July 2025 Pulse Survey found that US executive education programs saw an 18% decrease in both custom and open enrollment demand — the steepest decline of any region globally. At the same time, more than half of exec ed leaders report increased pressure from institutional leadership on revenue and financial performance.

challengeBullets.title: Sponsors are cutting programs they cannot measure
challengeBullets.body: L&D budgets are under review everywhere. When sponsors cannot see evidence of skill change, renewal is a negotiation — not a given. Completion certificates and satisfaction scores are no longer enough to justify a six-figure investment.

challengeBullets.title: The new buying question is "what changed?"
challengeBullets.body: Corporate buyers are not asking whether participants finished. They are asking if they can apply newly learned skills. If your program cannot answer that question with data, a buyer will find one that can.

challengeBullets.title: Internal academies are closing the differentiation gap
challengeBullets.body: Corporate universities now offer many of the same topics as executive programs, but faster and cheaper. The programs that retain sponsor relationships are the ones that deliver something internal teams cannot: verified, third-party skill evidence tied to real work.

howItWorksSubheader: Peerceptiv works inside your existing courses — using the projects, presentations, and capstones you already assign — to surface real skill data you can bring to every sponsor conversation.
ctaHeading: See what Peerceptiv can do for Berkeley's Programs
ctaBody: 15 minutes. We'll show you exactly how it works for executive cohorts and answer any questions about fit.

headline: JPMorgan Develops the Best Analysts. Now You Can Prove It.
subheading: JPMorgan Chase runs some of the most competitive early career programs in financial services. Peerceptiv helps you measure whether your analysts are actually ready — not just whether they showed up.
challengeHeader: The Problem With Manager-Centric Assessment at Scale
challengeIntro: When early career cohorts span Investment Banking, Wealth Management, Commercial Banking, and Technology — each with its own managing directors and VPs reviewing project work — assessment becomes inconsistent by design. In organizations your size, a consistent pattern emerges: some analysts get rigorous, detailed feedback. Others get a checkbox. And no one can answer the question that matters most at promotion time.

challengeBullets.title: 50+ Business Lines. 50+ Different Grading Standards.
challengeBullets.body: When every managing director evaluates Summer Analysts and early career cohorts against their own internal bar, you don't have a feedback system — you have a lottery. Structured peer review enforces a consistent rubric across every division, every cohort, every geography.

challengeBullets.title: You Can See Completion. You Can't See Readiness.
challengeBullets.body: JPMorgan Chase's early career programs are built around real work — deal analysis, client case projects, analytics deliverables. That's exactly the kind of high-stakes output that deserves rigorous evaluation. But 'completed the project' and 'has the judgment to advise clients' are two very different data points. Right now, you're measuring one of them.

challengeBullets.title: Your Managing Directors Are Doing Two Jobs.
challengeBullets.body: Reviewing 30+ project submissions per cohort cycle is not a high-value use of a VP or MD's time. At firms like Accenture — running 1,500+ licenses across structured cohorts — Peerceptiv reduced grading burden by 85%. Peer review handles the volume. Your managers focus on development conversations that actually build the leadership pipeline.

howItWorksSubheader: Structured peer review that works inside JPMorgan Chase's competency framework — not instead of it.
ctaHeading: See What Peerceptiv Can Do for JPMorgan Chase's Programs
ctaBody: Peerceptiv doesn't replace your competency model or your managers. It gives you the measurement infrastructure to prove — with data — that your early career talent is ready for the next level. If you're evaluating how to scale consistent, rigorous assessment across your analyst and associate cohorts, let's talk. Accenture saw 35% better learning outcomes and 85% less grading time. Here's what that looks like in your context.

companyName: Yale School of Management - Executive Education
eyebrow: Prepared for Yale School of Management - Executive Education
headline: Your Sponsors Are Paying for Behavior Change. Are You Measuring It?
subheading: Peerceptiv turns the real work inside your executive programs into structured skill evidence, giving sponsors ROI data they demand and your program a competitive advantage.
challengeHeader: The Gap Between What Yale Teaches and Can Prove
challengeIntro: When a corporate sponsor invests $50K+ per seat in a Yale SOM program, they expect their leaders to return ready to drive transformation — not just report a high NPS. The challenge is capturing behaviour change and ROI data within the program itself.

challengeBullets.title: Sponsor Renewals Hinge on Proof, Not Satisfaction
challengeBullets.body: Post-program surveys tell sponsors their executives enjoyed Yale. They don't tell sponsors which leaders can apply strategic frameworks under pressure, facilitate a transformation, or lead through ambiguity. When renewal conversations happen, 'would recommend' ratings aren't enough — behavior evidence is.

challengeBullets.title: Digital Cohort Expansion Creates an Assessment Gap
challengeBullets.body: As Yale SOM scales digital and blended delivery, grading and peer feedback across distributed cohorts becomes labor-intensive and inconsistent. Instructor evaluation alone can't capture how executives perform in the eyes of their peers — and that's exactly the data corporate sponsors are starting to ask for.

challengeBullets.title: Competitors Are Moving Toward Evidence-Based Outcomes
challengeBullets.body: Wharton, INSEAD, and boutique providers are all competing for the same corporate relationships. Programs that can demonstrate measurable skill application — not just completion — will win sponsor loyalty. Structured peer assessment is differentiation competitors can't easily copy.

howItWorksSubheader: Peerceptiv works inside your existing courses — using the projects, presentations, and capstones you already assign — to surface real skill data you can bring to every sponsor conversation.
ctaHeading: See What Peerceptiv Can Do for Yale School of Management Executive Education's Programs
ctaBody: Organizations running cohort-based programs report 35% improved learning outcomes and 85% less time spent on evaluation. Let's show you how Yale SOM Executive Education can turn every cohort project into sponsor-ready proof of applied capability.

headline: Your Early-Career Programs Are Rigorous. Your Skill Data Should Be Too.
subheading: Prudential Financial runs structured, multi-cohort early-career programs across Investments, Actuarial and beyond — but when 100+ associates complete rotations simultaneously, can you actually show which competencies are mastered and which are still at risk? Peerceptiv gives you that answer.
challengeHeader: The Evaluation Gap Hiding Inside Well-Run Programs
challengeIntro: Firms we work with in financial services consistently hit the same wall: they can report completion and satisfaction, but not skill mastery. Here's where the friction shows up.

challengeBullets.title: 30 managers. 100+ associates. No consistent signal.
challengeBullets.body: When associates are distributed across programs and evaluated by different managers, feedback quality varies — not because managers aren't capable, but because there's no shared rubric anchoring 'analytical rigor' or 'risk judgment' across cohorts. The result: your exec team gets feedback volume, not skill evidence.

challengeBullets.title: You can answer 'did they complete it?' — not 'are they ready?'
challengeBullets.body: Rotation sign-offs and program surveys tell you engagement happened. They don't tell you which associates have mastered investment analysis or where competency gaps are clustered across your cohort. When leadership asks 'are they ready for the next rotation?' — what data do you have now...

challengeBullets.title: SME review burden scales with every new cohort.
challengeBullets.body: With continuous recruiting into early-talent programs, every new cohort adds to the evaluation load on your senior associates and managers. That's a retention and burnout risk hiding in plain sight — and it gets worse as programs grow, not better.

howItWorksSubheader: Structured peer review — anchored to your competency rubrics — handles 80% of the evaluation work, surfaces mastery data by competency across every associate and cohort, and frees your managers to coach instead of grade. A financial services proof point: 96% competency mastery achieved at a Fortune 100 insurer using this exact model.
ctaHeading: See what Peerceptiv can do for Prudential Financial's Programs
ctaBody:
We'll show you exactly how Peerceptiv fits into your existing rotation and evaluation structure, with a live look at the skill heatmaps and competency dashboards. 

Book a 15-minute walkthrough.

headline: BlackRock Measures Everything. What About Early Career Mastery?
subheading: BlackRock's early career analysts work on real investment, risk, and analytics problems from day one. Peerceptiv tells you — with data — which ones have actually mastered the competencies that matter.
challengeHeader: The Gap Between 'Program Completed' and 'Ready to Perform'
challengeIntro: At BlackRock's scale — with early career talent distributed across divisions, geographies, and business units — running a consistent, rigorous assessment process is operationally hard. Most programs can answer 'did they complete it?' What they can't answer is 'are they ready?' That's the gap that shows up in month 8, not month 1.

challengeBullets.title: Manager Bandwidth Is a Bottleneck
challengeBullets.body: When analysts and associates submit real project work — portfolio analyses, risk assessments, client presentations — someone has to review it. Across dozens of managers and multiple divisions, that review burden accumulates fast. Managers end up grading submissions instead of coaching talent.

challengeBullets.title: Feedback Consistency Breaks Down at Scale
challengeBullets.body: With early career evaluation distributed across business units from Asset Management to Aladdin to iShares, there's no guarantee a cohort in London gets the same quality of feedback as one in New York. Without a shared rubric framework, 'strong performer' means something different in every room.

challengeBullets.title: No Unified View of Readiness for Leadership
challengeBullets.body: BlackRock's exec team can see portfolio performance to the basis point. But when a business unit leader asks 'which of our analysts are ready to move up?' — what does your data actually show? Completion rates and manager impressions aren't a readiness answer. A skill heatmap by competency is.

howItWorksSubheader: Structured Peer Review That Scales Across Every Division and Cohort
ctaHeading: See What Peerceptiv Can Do for BlackRock's Programs
ctaBody: Accenture deployed Peerceptiv across 1,500 licenses to solve exactly this problem — distributed cohorts, real work deliverables, and a leadership team that needed skill proof, not completion data. If you're ready to see what that looks like for BlackRock's early career programs, let's talk. No pitch deck — just a focused conversation about where your current process has gaps and whether we can fill them.

headline: Build and Measure Future Ready Skills in Early Career and HiPo Programs
subheading: Aptiv develops emerging talent across 50+ countries. Peerceptiv provides the platform to apply and measure in-demand skills for a future-ready workforce.
challengeHeader: The Gap Between 'They Completed It' and 'They're Ready'
challengeIntro: You've built a metrics-driven talent strategy that connects people investment to enterprise value. But when it comes to early careers and emerging talent, the data most platforms give you stops at completion — not capability. That gap is a board-level risk.

challengeBullets.title: Managers across 50 countries can't scale consistent development
challengeBullets.body: When every manager reviews work-quality differently, your early career cohorts don't get equitable development — they get whoever happened to be their reviewer that quarter. At Aptiv's scale, that inconsistency compounds quickly.

challengeBullets.title: Most can answer 'did they finish?' but not 'are they ready to lead?'
challengeBullets.body: Your board-level narrative demands KPIs that connect learning to performance. Course completions don't give you that. Without competency-level mastery data, it's nearly impossible to answer the question that matters: which emerging talent is actually ready for the next level?

challengeBullets.title: Regrettable attrition starts when high-potential talent doesn't feel developed
challengeBullets.body: Early career professionals leave when they don't see growth. Generic feedback from overloaded managers — or no feedback at all — signals that development isn't real. Structured peer review gives every participant rigorous, rubric-based feedback that feels like investment, not checkbox.

howItWorksSubheader: Peerceptiv replaces inconsistent manager grading with structured peer assessment — so every emerging talent at Aptiv gets calibrated, rubric-based feedback, and you get a competency heatmap that finally answers: who's ready?
ctaHeading: See What Peerceptiv Can Do for Aptiv's Programs
ctaBody: Book a 30-minute session to see how Peerceptiv gives Aptiv's early careers program the ability to apply key skills, plus the board-ready narrative to prove your talent investment is building the pipeline you're promising.

headline: 84% of sales training content is forgotten within 3 months
subheading: Peerceptiv helps apply training into day-to-day work & prove that training actually changes how sellers compete — not just that they completed the modules.
challengeHeader: The gap current training measurement can't close
challengeIntro: You're running one of the most complex sales enablement programs in enterprise tech — 10,000+ sellers, technical specialists, and channel partners across every major region, with a multimillion-dollar investment behind it. The content is strong. The rollout is real. The problem is what you can't prove when someone asks if it's working.

challengeBullets.title: 'Certified' doesn't = ready to sell.
challengeBullets.body: When a seller completes your AI or Data Center training, you know they finished it. Do you know if they can articulate why Lenovo beats Dell on inference latency under pressure, or if they can build the business case that moves a $2M infrastructure deal forward?

challengeBullets.title: 'Ready to sell' means something different in Singapore than in Austin.
challengeBullets.body: At 10,000+ learners across geographies and partner ecosystems, evaluation consistency is a structural problem. Without standardized rubrics and calibrated review, your competency bar shifts by region, by facilitator, and by program tier — and no one has defensible data to show it doesn't.

challengeBullets.title: Make your high potential sellers visible
challengeBullets.body: Enterprise sales orgs Lenovo's size typically have sellers on a track toward account leadership — but identification is often based on quota attainment and manager instinct, not demonstrated strategic thinking or deal complexity. Peerceptiv gives you real readiness data.

howItWorksSubheader: Embed structured peer assessment directly into your training rollouts — same standard from Tokyo to Austin, at scale.
ctaHeading: See what Peerceptiv can do for Lenovo's programs.
ctaBody:
Lenovo is investing at the scale where 'we ran the training' is no longer enough. Let's show you how structured peer and executive assessment — built into your existing AI enablement programs — gives you defensible competency data across 10,000+ learners, without overhauling what's already working.
